summary: Physician-scientist with 16 years of experience in academic oncology and biopharma clinical development. Currently Vice President of Clinical Development at Bristol Myers Squibb, leading immuno-oncology programmes including checkpoint inhibitors and CAR-T cell therapy across Phase I-III trials. Previously Associate Professor of Medicine at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center. Track record includes 8 IND filings, 3 NDA/BLA submissions, and 35+ peer-reviewed publications. Board-certified in Medical Oncology (ABIM).

experience.title: Vice President, Clinical Development — Immuno-Oncology
bullets: Led clinical development strategy for 3 immuno-oncology programs spanning checkpoint inhibitors and CAR-T cell therapy across Phase I through Phase III
Built and managed clinical development team of 22 including medical directors, clinical scientists, and medical monitors
Oversaw 5 IND filings and 2 supplemental BLA submissions, all accepted by FDA without clinical hold
Presented pivotal trial data at ASCO Annual Meeting (2022, 2023) and AACR Annual Meeting (2021)
Served as medical monitor for registrational Phase III trial enrolling 1,200 patients across 180 global sites

experience.title: Associate Professor of Medicine — Solid Tumor Oncology
bullets: Conducted investigator-initiated clinical trials in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and melanoma
Published 28 peer-reviewed papers including 12 as first or senior author in Journal of Clinical Oncology, NEJM, and Lancet Oncology
Served as site principal investigator for 6 industry-sponsored Phase I-II trials in solid tumor oncology
Mentored 8 oncology fellows and 3 post-doctoral researchers in clinical and translational research
Served on MSK's Institutional Review Board (IRB) for 4 years

experience.title: Medical Oncology Fellow
bullets: Completed 3-year fellowship in Medical Oncology with research focus on tumor immunology
Awarded NIH K08 Career Development Award for translational immuno-oncology research

clinical_development: Phase I-III trial design and execution
